title: When Credit Is Useful
fullRewardHint: Answer all {{total}} choices correctly to earn the full reward.
reflectionPrompts: How can you distinguish between needs and wants when considering credit?
What criteria should you use to evaluate borrowing decisions?

options.label: For impulse spending
options.reflection: Borrowing for impulse purchases often leads to debt problems and financial stress without clear benefits.

options.label: For luxury items you can't afford
options.reflection: Using credit for unaffordable luxuries typically creates financial strain rather than genuine value.

options.label: Anytime you want something
options.reflection: Borrowing without purpose or repayment planning often leads to accumulating debt problems.

options.label: For planned needs with repayment capacity
options.reflection: Exactly! Borrowing is reasonable when it's for planned needs and you have the capacity to repay comfortably.

stages.prompt: What makes a borrowing decision financially sound?

options.label: Strong desire to own something immediately
options.reflection: Desire alone doesn't make borrowing sound - financial capacity and planning are essential.

options.label: Social pressure to keep up appearances
options.reflection: Borrowing due to social pressure often leads to financial difficulties and regret.

options.label: Ability to repay without financial stress
options.reflection: Exactly! Sound borrowing requires confirming you can repay comfortably within your budget.

options.label: Making only minimum monthly payments
options.reflection: Minimum payments often extend debt terms and increase total costs significantly.

stages.prompt: Which situation shows responsible credit use?

options.label: Using credit cards for unexpected medical bills
options.reflection: Emergency situations like medical bills are appropriate uses of credit when you have a repayment plan.

options.label: Charging an expensive vacation you can't afford
options.reflection: Vacations should be saved for rather than financed through credit that creates long-term debt.

options.label: Buying clothes on credit during sales
options.reflection: Sales don't make unaffordable purchases responsible - credit should fund planned needs, not wants.

options.label: Financing the latest gadgets on installment plans
options.reflection: Gadgets depreciate quickly, making installment financing financially inefficient for most people.

stages.prompt: How should you evaluate if credit will support stability?

options.label: Ignore the total cost and focus on monthly payments
options.reflection: Focusing only on monthly payments hides the true cost and can lead to unsustainable debt accumulation.

options.label: Hope your income will increase to cover payments
options.reflection: Relying on uncertain future income increases financial risk and can lead to payment difficulties.

options.label: Calculate total cost including interest and your ability to repay
options.reflection: Exactly! Responsible credit use requires calculating total costs and ensuring repayment fits your financial plan.

options.label: Compare offers but not your actual repayment ability
options.reflection: Comparing offers is helpful, but confirming your repayment ability is essential for financial stability.

stages.prompt: What should credit support rather than create?

options.label: Bad spending habits
options.reflection: Credit should not enable poor financial habits - it should support responsible financial behavior.

options.label: An unaffordable lifestyle
options.reflection: Using credit to maintain an unaffordable lifestyle creates financial stress and long-term problems.

options.label: Impulse buying decisions
options.reflection: Credit should facilitate planned financial decisions, not enable spontaneous impulse purchases.

options.label: Financial stability and planned goals
options.reflection: Exactly! Credit should support stability by enabling planned purchases and financial goals.
